feed.title: Jeff Bezos' Amazon Urges FCC To Prevent Elon Musk's SpaceX Proposal To Launch A Million Satellites
feed.summary: Amazon has filed a formal objection with the FCC against SpaceX's proposal to launch a million satellites, citing concerns about its feasibility, potential orbital congestion, and lack of detailed plans. The company argues that the project is more speculative than practical, highlighting the unlikelihood of deploying such a massive constellation given current launch rates. This move intensifies the rivalry between Amazon and SpaceX in the satellite internet market.

feed.title: Campbell’s Soup factory in Paris to lay off 205 workers

Soup production will cease on May 1st, and employees who remain until their layoff date will be eligible for separation benefits. The factory has been operating since 1964.

feed.title: H&R Block made a mistake on my taxes. Why won’t it help me?
feed.summary: Lance Croft faced a $14,000 IRS penalty because his H&R Block tax preparer missed a page of his retirement income. Despite H&R Block's 100% Accuracy Guarantee and initial admissions of error, Croft's claim was repeatedly denied. After intervention from a consumer advocate, H&R Block reversed its decision and agreed to provide the maximum refund under its Peace of Mind guarantee.

feed.title: IHS Holding Buyout By MTN Turns Tower Growth Story Into Cash Exit

The acquisition, valued at approximately US$6.2 billion, offers shareholders immediate liquidity at a premium, providing an exit path amidst challenging market conditions for tower operators. The transaction must still navigate regulatory approvals and integration challenges, with investors now focusing on the certainty of closing and payout timing rather than future tenancy growth.

feed.title: Dozens protest Citizens Bank over ICE ties
feed.summary: Dozens of protesters gathered outside several Citizens Bank branches in Cambridge, Massachusetts, to protest the company's alleged financial support for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The demonstrations in Harvard Square and Central Square are part of a larger nationwide movement critiquing the bank's ties to federal immigration forces. NewsCenter 5 has reached out to Citizens Bank for a comment on the allegations.

feed.title: JEF ALERT: Kaplan Fox is Investigating Jefferies Financial Group Inc. (JEF) for Potential Securities Law Violations
feed.summary: Kaplan Fox & Kilsheimer LLP is investigating Jefferies Financial Group Inc. (JEF) for potential securities law violations following reports from The Financial Times. The investigation stems from Jefferies' relationship with collapsed car parts company First Brands, including its role in invoice financing and advising the company. Jefferies' stock prices fell significantly after reports surfaced about its exposure to First Brands invoices, an SEC investigation, and a $30 million loss related to the company's collapse.

feed.title: LAKELAND INDUSTRIES Deadline to Lead in Securities Class Action Lawsuit is April 24, 2026 - Contact Kaplan Fox

(NASDAQ: LAKE) for investors who purchased securities between December 1, 2023, and December 9, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Lakeland made false and misleading statements regarding its financial health and the success of its acquisitions, particularly after reporting a significant drop in net sales and withdrawing financial guidance. Investors who suffered losses have until April 24, 2026, to move the court to serve as a lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb's Sotyktu Approved for Psoriatic Arthritis Treatment
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb's oral drug Sotyktu has received FDA approval for treating adults with active psoriatic arthritis, offering a new treatment option for joint and skin symptoms. Clinical trials showed significant efficacy, with 54% of patients achieving an ACR20 response compared to placebo. This approval strengthens Sotyktu's role in psoriatic arthritis management and enhances Bristol Myers Squibb's standing in the immunology market.

This expansion aims to increase chicken production capacity and is expected to create 100 new jobs over the next five years, boosting the region's economy and manufacturing workforce. Construction is planned to begin in late 2026, reinforcing Conagra's commitment to the local community and its frozen foods business growth.

feed.title: Executive Summary: Global Retirement Savers Study
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price's 2025 Global Retirement Savers Study, which expanded from the U.S. to include Japan, Canada, the UK, and Australia, reveals that global retirement savers are highly concerned about inflation and exhibit low confidence in their retirement prospects. Key findings indicate mixed economic expectations, a preference for investment choice with professional guidance, and a significant reliance on workplace resources and human advisors for financial advice. The study also highlights a widespread expectation among savers to work during retirement, stemming from financial insecurity and the inability to define clear retirement timelines.

feed.title: NetEase has reportedly pulled funding from Yakuza creator Toshihiro Nagoshi's studio and next game Gang of Dragon

Nagoshi, known for his work on Yakuza/Like a Dragon, is now seeking new funding sources for his studio, which was established in 2021. If new funding isn't secured, the studio may be forced to close, potentially leaving the game unreleased.

The court awarded InMode $25.2 million in damages and issued a permanent injunction against the sellers. This ruling allows InMode to continue removing infringing listings and shut down fraudulent stores.

feed.title: CORT INVESTOR REMINDER: Corcept Therapeutics Incorporated Investors Have Until April 21, 2026 To Seek Lead Plaintiff Role
feed.summary: Corcept Therapeutics investors who suffered losses between October 31, 2024, and December 30, 2025, are reminded that they have until April 21, 2026, to seek lead plaintiff status in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Corcept misrepresented the likelihood of FDA approval for its hypercortisolism drug, relacorilant, despite the FDA repeatedly raising concerns about the adequacy of clinical evidence. The stock price declined significantly after the FDA issued a Complete Response Letter on December 31, 2025, and further after the CRL's details were published on January 30, 2026.

feed.title: State regulators give tentative approval to Aquarion Water Co. sale amid concerns over rates, oversight
feed.summary: Connecticut's Public Utilities Regulatory Authority (PURA) has given preliminary approval to the acquisition of Aquarion Water Co. by the South Central Connecticut Regional Water Authority, despite concerns from opponents regarding governance, rate-setting, and rate impact. This decision comes after PURA initially rejected the merger, which was subsequently challenged in court, leading to a judicial order for reconsideration. Critics, including Attorney General William Tong, argue that the deal will lead to significant transaction costs and annual water rate hikes for consumers.

feed.title: State regulators give tentative approval to Aquarion Water Co. sale amid concerns over rates, oversight
feed.summary: Connecticut's Public Utilities Regulatory Authority (PURA) has given preliminary approval to the acquisition of Aquarion Water Co. by the South Central Connecticut Regional Water Authority, despite concerns over governance, rate oversight, and potential rate increases. The decision comes after a previous rejection by PURA and a subsequent court review, which sent the case back for further consideration. Opponents, including Attorney General William Tong, criticize the ruling as an "economic disaster" for Connecticut, citing potential transaction costs and rate hikes, while proponents argue it's a constructive step benefiting customers and communities.

feed.title: CT man pleads guilty to defrauding Amazon out of $3.5M with false transportation assignments
feed.summary: Ameer Nasir, 25, of Trumbull, Connecticut, pleaded guilty to wire fraud, admitting to defrauding Amazon out of over $3.5 million. Nasir registered 23 fake trucking businesses, including one under his company's name and others fraudulently using real company identities, to misrepresent that he had completed more than 1,000 transportation assignments for Amazon Logistics between December 2019 and February 2021. He manipulated Amazon's transportation management system and submitted fraudulent invoices, agreeing to pay restitution and facing a maximum of 20 years in prison.

feed.title: Uber Air Taxis In Dubai Test Multi Modal Platform Ambitions
feed.summary: Uber Technologies is partnering with Joby Aviation to launch an all-electric air taxi service in Dubai, integrated directly into the Uber app for combined air and ground transport bookings. This move represents Uber's ambition to expand beyond traditional ride-hailing into multi-modal transportation and leverage future mobility trends. While it's a test of Uber's platform for higher-priced, premium travel without the risk of aircraft ownership, investors will be watching its success as a "proof of concept" rather than an immediate revenue driver.

feed.title: Does Onshape’s New Cloud MBD Capability Deepen PTC’s (PTC) Role in End‑to‑End Product Lifecycles?
feed.summary: PTC Inc. has introduced a new cloud-native Model-Based Definition (MBD) capability within its Onshape CAD and PDM platform. This feature allows engineering teams to embed manufacturing information directly into 3D models, aiming to centralize product and manufacturing data in a single cloud-native model. The initiative is intended to deepen PTC's role in customers' end-to-end product lifecycles and support AI-driven process improvements, although the article notes it doesn't immediately alter the focus on SaaS transition or mitigate competitive pressures.

feed.title: Brazil Steel Industry: Gerdau Warns of Import Pressure, Seeks Trade Measures - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Gerdau, a leading Brazilian steelmaker, has voiced concerns over the escalating volume of steel imports, projecting they will exceed six million tons in 2025, constituting nearly 30% of domestic sales. The company warns that without protective trade measures, many firms could face financial distress and operational struggles. Gerdau is optimistic about improved government attention to this issue and anticipates a market expansion driven by growth in the automotive sector and sustained construction activity, aligning with their recent investment in restarting a billet production facility.

feed.title: France's top court upholds Criteo's €40M GDPR fine - but the legal logic is contested
feed.summary: France's highest administrative court, the Conseil d'État, has upheld a €40 million GDPR fine against advertising technology company Criteo. The fine, originally issued by the French data protection authority CNIL in 2023, addresses multiple violations including lack of valid consent proof, inadequate information to users, incomplete data erasure practices, and insufficient joint controller agreements. While the court confirmed the violations, legal experts are disputing the evidentiary reasoning used to justify the full fine amount, particularly regarding the determination that all 370 million identifiers processed by Criteo constitute personal data.

The stock's omission occurred even after SoFi reported strong Q4 2025 results, with record revenue exceeding $1 billion. Retail investors on Stocktwits expressed bullish sentiment, believing the company's continued growth and future "blowout" earnings will eventually lead to S&P 500 inclusion.

feed.title: Assessing Restaurant Brands International (QSR) Valuation After Recent Share Price Strength
feed.summary: Restaurant Brands International (QSR) has seen recent share price strength, prompting a re-evaluation of its valuation. The stock is currently trading at $73.98, with a narrative fair value of $77.76, suggesting it is undervalued by approximately 4.9%. Despite a P/E ratio higher than industry averages, the company's rapid international expansion and digital investments are highlighted as drivers for future growth and higher long-term earnings visibility.

feed.title: Dentsply Sirona gains as BofA upgrades to buy on risk-reward setup
feed.summary: Dentsply Sirona (NASDAQ: XRAY) shares are rising following an upgrade from BofA to Buy from Neutral, with a price target increase to $42 from $37. The upgrade is based on an attractive risk-reward setup, reflecting that the company has likely reset expectations. BofA anticipates that the core dental market will recover in 2024 and 2025.

feed.title: Insider Sell: James Reske Sells Shares of First Commonwealth Fin
feed.summary: James Reske, CFO of First Commonwealth Financial Corp (FCF), sold 2,072 shares of the company on March 5, 2026, reducing his total holdings to 85,492 shares. Over the past year, Reske has sold 16,269 shares and the company has seen 6 insider buys against 10 insider sells. The stock is currently priced at $17.58, with a market cap of $1.77 billion, and is considered fairly valued based on its GF Value.

feed.title: NI Holdings, Inc. Reports Results for Fourth Quarter and Year Ended December 31, 2025
feed.summary: NI Holdings, Inc. reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. The company experienced declines in direct written premiums and net earned premiums, primarily due to a strategic reduction in the Non-Standard Auto segment. Despite a challenging year, marked by unfavorable prior year reserve development and a historic catastrophe event in North Dakota, the company is focusing on its core businesses and anticipates a return to profitability in 2026.

feed.title: According to the latest SEC filing, Nortech Systems Incorporated (Nortech) has made significant amendments to its existing credit agreement.
feed.summary: Nortech Systems Incorporated has amended its credit agreement, introducing a new EBITDA financial threshold and increasing interest rates for various loan types. These changes reflect lenders' heightened risk control and will impact Nortech's financing costs and future cash flow management. The adjustment highlights how financial market conditions influence corporate financing.

feed.title: Is Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) Pricing Look Attractive After Recent Share Price Pullback
feed.summary: Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) has experienced recent share price declines but appears undervalued based on two key valuation approaches. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests a 57.9% discount to its intrinsic value, while its current P/E ratio of 16.7x is below both industry and peer averages, further indicating undervaluation. The article encourages investors to use valuation narratives on their platform to form their own assessment of the company's future prospects.

feed.title: Insider Sell Alert: Peter Mccann Sells 4,000 Shares of Civeo Cor
feed.summary: Peter Mccann, Senior Vice President of Australia at Civeo Corp (CVEO), sold 4,000 shares of the company on March 6, 2026. This transaction leaves him with 50,942 shares and is part of a trend of insider selling at Civeo Corp, with no insider buys in the past year. Shares were trading at $28.64, suggesting the stock is modestly overvalued compared to its GF Value of $25.60.

feed.title: Nuveen Multi-Asset Income Fund VP buys $1,008 in stock
feed.summary: William A. Siffermann, VP of Nuveen Multi-Asset Income Fund (NMAI), recently bought 75 shares for $13.44 each, totaling $1,008, bringing his direct ownership to 330.0972 shares. The fund offers a 10.55% dividend yield, has a 19% total return over the past year, and a "GREAT" Financial Health score, according to InvestingPro. Additionally, the fund has announced a change in its portfolio management team with three new managers appointed.

feed.title: Nuvve Partners with OMNIA Global to Address 1GW Development Pipeline with First 50MW/75MWh Battery Energy Storage System in Sweden
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. has partnered with OMNIA Global to develop a 1 GW pipeline of battery energy storage systems (BESS) over the next two years, starting with a 50MW/75MWh BESS in Sweden. This collaboration expands Nuvve's European footprint and aims to generate significant revenue, with the first project expected to be operational by Q2 2026. Nuvve will serve as the owner and asset manager, optimizing revenue through market access services and deploying its advanced software and V2G platform.

feed.title: Brookfield Real Assets Income Fund Inc. Announces Portfolio Management Team Change
feed.summary: Brookfield Public Securities Group LLC announced a change to the portfolio management team for the Brookfield Real Assets Income Fund Inc. (NYSE: RA). Effective June 30, 2026, Paula Horn will depart as Co-Portfolio Manager, while Gaal Surugeon, Chris Janus, and Riley O’Neil will continue in their roles, leveraging Brookfield's global resources for investment decisions.

feed.title: NEXTracker Welcomes Jeffrey Guldner to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Nextracker Inc. has appointed Jeffrey Guldner, a leader with extensive experience in the utility sector, as a Class III director and member of the Nominating, Governance and Public Responsibility Committee. Guldner's background includes executive roles at Pinnacle West Capital Corporation and legal expertise, and his independent status meets Nasdaq's listing rules. His appointment is expected to strengthen Nextracker's governance without reported conflicts of interest.

feed.title: Flushing Financial Investor Alert: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC Investigates Adequacy of Price and Process in Proposed Sale of Flushing Financial Corp. - FFIC
feed.summary: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF) is investigating the proposed sale of Flushing Financial Corp. (NasdaqGS: FFIC) to OceanFirst Financial Corp. The law firm is scrutinizing whether the offered consideration of 0.85 shares of OceanFirst common stock per Flushing Financial share and the process leading to this offer adequately value the company for its shareholders. KSF invites affected shareholders to contact them to discuss their legal rights regarding the proposed transaction.

feed.title: Employee commitment drives company success, Origin Bancorp asserts
feed.summary: Origin Bancorp emphasizes the crucial role of employee commitment in achieving company success and recognizes its team on National Employee Appreciation Day. The company actively fosters a positive workplace culture, aligns with initiatives for personal financial streamlining, and highlights its dedication to diversity, as seen in its recognition of influential women in finance. Origin Bancorp encourages job seekers to explore career opportunities within its supportive and purpose-driven environment.
